REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, AMENDED NOTICE OF SPECIAL DEMURRER AND SPECIAL DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT DECLINES TO RESOLVE THE DISPUTE REGARDING INTERPRETATION OF THE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS OR WHETHER SECTION 12 CONSTITUTES A RESTRAINT ON ALIENATION, ON DEMURRER. WITH RESPECT TO THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, THE COURT FINDS THAT TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF SEEKS A DECLARATION OF JUDGEMENT THAT DEFENDANT VIOLATED SECTION 12 OF THE ALLEGEDLY INCORPORATED TIC AGREEMENT, THAT CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. (MAGUIRE V. HIBERNIA SAV. & LOAN SOC'Y (1944) 23 CAL. 2D 719.) HOWEVER, TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS A DECLARATION OF RIGHTS -SPECIFICALLY, THAT DEFENDANTS MUST OBTAIN UNANIMOUS CONSENT BEFORE TRANSFERRING OWNERSHIP INTEREST TO THEIR CONDOMINIUM UNIT AND THAT THEY MUST COMPLY WITH THE RIGHT OF FIRST REFUSAL, (PRAYER FOR RELIEF, NOS. 4, 5), THE CLAIM IS NOT BARRED. (UNITED PACIFIC-RELIANCE INS. CO. V. DIDOMENICO (1985) 173 CAL.APP.3D 673, 667.) PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE ADDITIONAL FACTS CONCERNING ACCRUAL, TOLLING OR THE CONTINUING NATURE OF THE DISPUTE, AS REQUESTED IN THE OPPOSITION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) VISITING JUDGE: DENNIS HAYASHI ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY, (TRANSACTION ID # 78271388): VILTMAN, VLADIE P. SUBSTITUTED FOR MURRY, GEOFFREY AS ATTORNEY FOR SOMERS, RONALD

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. AS APTLY STATED BY DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL, "THERE IS NOTHING TO ARBITRATE YET." THE MATTER SHOULD BE SUBMITTED TO ARBITRATION "IF THE TIME ARRIVES," SUCH TIME UNDER SECTION 3.3 IS "IF A VOTE ON ANY MATTER IS NOT UNANIMOUS." IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT THERE HAS BEEN NO "VOTE," THAT PLAINTIFF REFUSES TO VOTE (SEE ZIDER FEBRUARY 17, 2026 DECLARATION) AND THAT DEFENDANT DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE PREPARED TO COMPEL THE VOTE. (SEE YANG DECLARATION, RESPONSE TO QUESTION 4 NOT PROVIDING AN ANSWER IN THE FORMAT REQUESTED BY THE COURT "E.G. "PARTY WILL TAKE STEPS TO COMPEL THE VOTE; THE ESTIMATED TIME IS 90 DAYS." PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-26-2026 Continued To Jul-22-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Amended Notice Of Special Demurrer And Special Demurrer To The Fourth Cause Of Action Of Plaintiffs' Complaint
Off Calendar May-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT RONALD SOMERS, JOEL YANG Amended Notice Of Special Demurrer And Special Demurrer To The Fourth Cause Of Action Of Plaintiffs' Complaint is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. The Court declines to resolve the dispute regarding interpretation of the governing documents or whether Section 12 constitutes a restraint on alienation, on demurrer. With respect to the statute of limitations, the Court finds that to the extent Plaintiff seeks a declaration of judgement that Defendant violated Section 12 of the allegedly incorporated TIC agreement, that claim is barred by the Statute of Limitations. (Maguire v. Hibernia Sav. & Loan Soc'y (1944) 23 Cal. 2d 719.) However, to the extent that Plaintiff seeks a declaration of rights -specifically, that Defendants must obtain unanimous consent before transferring ownership interest to their condominium unit and that they must comply with the right of first refusal, (Prayer for Relief, Nos. 4, 5), the claim is not barred. (United Pacific-Reliance Ins. Co. v. DiDomenico (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 673, 667.) Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege additional facts concerning accrual, tolling or the continuing nature of the dispute, as requested in the opposition.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Visiting Judge: Dennis Hayashi ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Amended Notice Of Special Demurrer And Special Demurrer To The Fourth Cause Of Action Of Plaintiffs' Complaint

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Special Demurrer And Special Demurrer To The Fourth Cause Of Action Of Plaintiffs' Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

Defendants' Demurrer to Fourth Cause of Action is OFF CALENDAR. Re-noticed for April 24, 2026.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Mar-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Paul Lee, Esq. representing Plaintiff Linda Zider via Zoom Video Webinar.
Vladie P. Viltman, Esq. representing Defendant Ronald Somers.

Hearing held.

Defendant's counsel argues as to the tentative ruling by offer of proof.
Plaintiff's counsel responds to defendant's counsel and submits to the Court's ruling.

After hearing the arguments, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION;

is DENIED. As aptly stated by defendant's counsel, "there is nothing to arbitrate yet." The matter should be submitted to arbitration "if the time arrives," such time under Section 3.3 is "if a vote on any matter is not unanimous." It is undisputed that there has been no "vote," that Plaintiff refuses to vote (See Zider February 17, 2026 Declaration) and that Defendant does not appear to be prepared to compel the vote. (See Yang Declaration, response to question 4 not providing an answer in the format requested by the Court "e.g. "party will take steps to compel the vote; the estimated time is 90 days."

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-11-2026 Continued To Apr-29-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Feb-09-2026 Continued To Mar-09-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Paul Lee, Esq. representing Plaintiff Linda Zider via Zoom Video Webinar.
Vladislav Viltman, Esq. representing Defendants.

Hearing held.
The Court admonishes both counsels present as the tentative ruling was not carefully read and complied with.

The Court continues the matter for both parties to comply with the request of the court.

The tentative ruling is modified as follows:
The court continues the matter to March 9, 2026 at 9:30 a.m.. Both sides to submit declarations no later than February 20, 2026 stating: (1) whether the declarant agrees to participate in a vote; the answer shall be limited to "yes" or "no"; (2) if the answer to question (1) is "yes," list the issues for the vote (limited to the issues listed in the complaint that are subject to Section 3.3); (3) if the answer to question (1) is "yes," provide the timeline required to conduct the vote, e.g. the vote can be conducted immediately; or the appropriate procedure requires preliminary steps to be taken, therefore the vote can take place the earliest on "date x;" and (4) if the party believes the vote may be compelled by any appropriate means, the time required to accomplish this, e.g. "party will take steps to compel the vote; the estimated time is 90 days."

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)
